examples.title: First Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Implementation of First Fit algorithm for contiguous memory allocation
examples.explanation: The First Fit algorithm scans memory from the beginning and allocates the first hole that is large enough. It returns quickly but may create large leftover holes.

examples.title: Best Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Implementation of Best Fit algorithm to find smallest suitable hole
examples.code:
def best_fit(holes, process_size):
    """
    holes: list of available memory block sizes
    process_size: size of incoming process
    Returns: index of best hole, or -1 if not found
    """
    best_idx = -1
    min_remaining = float('inf')
    
    for i, hole in enumerate(holes):
        if hole >= process_size:
            remaining = hole - process_size
            if remaining < min_remaining:
                min_remaining = remaining
                best_idx = i
    
    if best_idx != -1:
        holes[best_idx] -= process_size  # Allocate part of hole
        if holes[best_idx] == 0:
            holes.pop(best_idx)  # Remove exhausted hole
    
    return best_idx

# Example usage:
holes = [30, 20, 15, 40]
process = 18
# Result: Allocates to 20KB hole (index 1), remaining: 2KB
# Result index: 1
examples.explanation: Best Fit searches all holes and selects the smallest one that can accommodate the process, minimizing wasted space but requiring full scan.

examples.title: Worst Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Implementation of Worst Fit algorithm that always allocates largest hole
examples.explanation: Worst Fit chooses the largest available hole hoping to leave sufficiently large remaining space for future large allocations.

examples.title: Memory Compaction Simulation
examples.description: Simulates memory compaction to combine all free holes
examples.code:
def compact_memory(memory_blocks):
    """
    memory_blocks: list of [size, allocated(bool)]
    Returns: compacted memory layout
    """
    # Step 1: Separate allocated and free blocks
    allocated = [b for b in memory_blocks if b[1]]
    free_blocks = [b[0] for b in memory_blocks if not b[1]]
    
    # Step 2: Calculate total free space
    total_free = sum(free_blocks)
    
    # Step 3: Create compacted layout
    compacted = allocated.copy()
    compacted.append([total_free, False])  # One large free block
    
    return compacted

# Example before compaction:
memory = [[30, True], [10, False], [25, True], 
          [15, False], [40, True], [30, False]]
# After: [[30, True], [25, True], [40, True], [150, False]]

print("Memory after compaction:", compact_memory(memory))
examples.explanation: Compaction moves all allocated blocks together, leaving one large contiguous free space. This eliminates external fragmentation but requires moving processes in memory.
